prompt:
Implement a FleetRide Dispatch & Billing Console for offline ride and carpool operations managed from a dispatcher’s desktop. The primary roles are Dispatcher (creates and manages trips and customer orders), Finance Clerk (reviews payments, refunds, invoices, and exports), and Administrator (configures policies and oversees audit/health). Built as a JavaFX desktop application, the UI must support Windows 11 with a baseline of 1920x1080 and high-DPI scaling, offering a multi-window workspace: a trip intake window, an order timeline window, and a settlement/reconciliation window that can be used in parallel. Desktop-first interactions are required, including right-click context menus on orders (Accept, Start Trip, Complete, Cancel, Open Dispute), global keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+N new trip, Ctrl+F search orders, Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+Shift+E export), and deep clipboard integration for copying addresses and order IDs. A system tray presence must allow “minimize to tray,” lock/unlock, and quick status indicators for overdue balances and jobs pending. Passengers are represented as local customer records; Dispatchers post ride requests with pickup/drop-off, rider count (1–6), time window (e.g., 03/27/2026 2:00 PM–3:00 PM), vehicle type, service priority, and optional floor level notes for pickup/drop-off. Orders must follow explicit states (pending match, accepted, in progress, completed, canceled, in dispute) with enforceable rules: auto-cancel if not accepted within 15 minutes, cancellation fees of $5.00 if canceled within 10 minutes of scheduled pickup, and dispute creation allowed up to 7 days after completion.

All data persists locally in a SQLite database with encrypted-at-rest fields for passwords and payment tokens (AES-256 with an admin-set local key) and masked display for sensitive identifiers (e.g., showing only last 4 characters). Pricing and settlement run fully offline through a configurable pricing engine: base fare $3.50 plus $1.80 per mile, $0.35 per minute, priority multiplier 1.25x, and floor-level surcharge of $1.00 per floor above 3; coupons support percent-off (max 20%) or fixed $ amount with a minimum order threshold (e.g., $25.00), and subsidies can be applied per customer with a monthly cap (default $50.00). Payment handling must be recorded as offline tenders (cash, card-on-file placeholder, check) while preserving the settlement ledger concepts: deposits (default 20%) and final payments, partial refunds, and reconciliation exports to CSV. File and asset management must save attachments (receipts, signed waivers, incident photos) to a controlled local directory with type allowlist (PDF/JPG/PNG), max 20 MB per file, SHA-256 fingerprinting, and basic format validation; access authorization is enforced by local roles and time-limited “share links” implemented as expiring local tokens (default 24 hours) that only work on the same machine. Administration includes a local configuration center for parameters, dictionaries, and templates, plus scheduled jobs (hourly timeout enforcement, nightly overdue/fee calculation, quota reclamation) with detailed logs, health checks, and an offline update mechanism that imports signed update packages with rollback to the prior version. The app must be stable for 30-day continuous operation, release file handles and DB connections deterministically, maintain a crash-safe checkpoint so in-progress state transitions resume without duplication (idempotent processing), and keep startup under 5 seconds on a standard office PC.

/eagle-initial
